visual_features.overall_style: Warm, playful, child-friendly design with a slightly retro, illustrated look. Rounded shapes, soft edges, and watercolor-style graphics create a welcoming atmosphere suitable for a municipal nursery/daycare. The style is modern in structure but uses nostalgic, handmade visual cues (illustrations, chunky display type) instead of a tech/corporate aesthetic.
visual_features.visual_hierarchy: Hierarchy is built with strong size contrast, color, and weight. The hero headline in the slider (top, partially visible) uses very large, all‑caps display type with a light outline, clearly the primary focal point. In the welcome section, the word 'Üdvözlet' is a large, bold, dark-brown display heading that stands out against the light cream background. Below it, a slightly smaller but still bold introductory line acts as a secondary heading. Body copy is regular-weight dark text. The vertical numbered circles (1, 2, 3) along the left side give immediate structure to the content, acting as visual anchors and sub‑section markers. The colorful illustration on the right (children and house) is a secondary focal point balancing the heavy typography column on the left.
visual_features.whitespace_usage: Sections are generously padded from the browser edges and between each other, creating a spacious, breathable layout. The text column has comfortable line length and sufficient line spacing, reducing visual clutter. Vertical spacing between the numbered paragraphs is ample, giving each bullet point its own visual block. The right illustration sits in its own space with enough breathing room around it, not crowded by text.
unique_elements: Large, chunky, rounded display headings reminiscent of children’s book titles.
Vertical numbered badges: circular yellow discs with centered white numerals, used to structure the welcome copy.
Watercolor-style illustration of children and a house with hearts and a star, giving a handcrafted, storybook feel.
Rounded-corner hero slider banner with large, outlined, 3D‑ish heading text.
Translucent rounded-square slider navigation arrows overlaid on the hero banner.
Small square scroll-to-top button in the bottom-right corner using the brand yellow/green tones.

layout_characteristics.grid_system: Appears to follow a centered, fixed-width container on a fluid background, likely based on a 12‑column grid. The welcome section uses a two‑column grid (approx. 7 columns text, 5 columns illustration).
layout_characteristics.layout_pattern: Top hero slider spanning the content width inside a rounded rectangle, followed by a full-width section with a centered two‑column layout (text on the left, illustration on the right). Classic top-to-bottom scroll with stacked sections.
layout_characteristics.section_organization: Sections are organized vertically with clear separation through background color consistency, padding, and large headings. Top: hero/slider with headline and controls. Below: 'Üdvözlet' welcome section with heading, intro text, and a numbered list in the left column, illustration in the right column.
layout_characteristics.alignment: Overall left-aligned text within a centered content container. Headings and paragraphs are left-aligned for readability. Numbered badges are aligned in a vertical column to the left of the paragraphs, with consistent horizontal alignment relative to the text block. The illustration on the right is visually centered in its column.
responsive_hints: Two-column layout suggests a breakpoint where columns would stack vertically on smaller screens (text first, illustration second).
Large typography and big tap targets (arrows, scroll button) imply mobile-friendliness.
Generous horizontal padding indicates content can shrink into a single centered column on narrow viewports without feeling cramped.
layout_characteristics.content_arrangement: Linear top-to-bottom flow: (1) A hero slider area at the top within a rounded rectangle. It contains a large headline and navigation arrows on the right overlay. (2) Immediately below, the 'Üdvözlet' section uses a two-column layout: left column contains a large heading ('Üdvözlet'), a bold introductory paragraph, then three numbered bullet paragraphs. Each bullet has a yellow circular badge (1–3) in a narrow sub-column on the far left with text occupying the main column to its right. Right column shows a single, large illustration of children in front of a house with hearts and a star. (3) A small scroll-to-top square button floats at the bottom-right of the viewport, independent of the content flow.
layout_characteristics.space_allocation: Within the welcome section, approximately 55–60% of the horizontal space is allocated to text (left), and 40–45% to the illustration (right). Vertically, the hero banner occupies the top portion with substantial padding and large text, then the welcome section fills most of the visible viewport height below. Within the text column, the heading and intro occupy the top 25–30%, while the numbered points take the rest with generous vertical gaps between them.
layout_characteristics.content_density: Moderately low density: the design favors readability and comfort over packing text. Font sizes are quite large, and paragraphs have good line height with visible spacing between segments. There is ample whitespace around the illustration and between list items, yielding an open, child-friendly editorial feel rather than an information-dense dashboard.
layout_characteristics.visual_flow: The eye follows a loose Z-pattern within the viewport: starting at the large hero headline near the top-left, moving across the hero to the right where the slider arrows live, then dropping down to the bold 'Üdvözlet' heading on the left. From there, the eye scans down through the vertical numbered circles and corresponding text, while intermittently pulled to the colorful illustration on the right which balances the composition and serves as a pleasant secondary focal area. The floating scroll-to-top button subtly anchors the bottom-right corner.

ui_patterns.visual_description: Buttons in this view are mainly navigation controls: the hero slider arrows and the scroll-to-top control. They appear as rounded rectangles or squares with soft corners (~8–10px radius). The slider buttons sit on top of the hero image inside semi-transparent beige or brownish overlays, with bold yellow arrow icons centered inside. They are flat or have very subtle inner shadows, consistent with a modern-flat aesthetic but with soft edges. The scroll-to-top button is a small square aligned to the bottom-right, filled with a yellow-green tone (similar to brand accent), containing a simple upward arrow icon in a lighter or white color. Padding around icons is generous, making them easy to tap.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Likely implemented as `<button>` or `<a>` elements with icon glyphs or inline SVGs (`<svg>` arrows) inside. For the slider: `<button class="slider-arrow slider-arrow--next">` with an `<span>` or `<svg>` arrow icon. For the scroll-to-top: `<a href="#top" class="scroll-top">` positioned fixed or sticky with CSS. Buttons probably share a base class defining padding, border-radius, and background, with modifier classes for color and state.

ui_patterns.visual_description: The most card-like element visible is the hero area: a large, rounded-rectangle panel with a light background (slightly darker than the page background) and possibly a soft drop shadow to lift it from the page. Corners are heavily rounded (~24–32px radius), giving a friendly, toy-like feeling. Content inside is edge-padded generously (24–32px). No strong borders; separation is achieved through contrast, shape, and shadow. Any additional cards elsewhere on the site are likely to follow this same pattern: soft rounded corners, light background, minimal border, and subtle shadow.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Typical structure would be a `<div class="card">` container, possibly with nested elements like `<div class="card__header">`, `<div class="card__body">`, and optional media elements. For the hero slider: `<div class="hero hero--slider">` containing heading text and navigation controls, each slide likely represented by a child container.

ui_patterns.visual_description: No explicit forms are visible in the screenshot, but based on the style, form inputs would likely use soft rounded rectangles, light cream or white fill, and thin, low-contrast borders (light brown/gray). Labels would probably be left-aligned above inputs for clarity. Focus states may use the brand yellow or a slightly darker border. Error/success states may color borders or small helper text in semantic colors. Density would be moderate with comfortable vertical spacing between fields to align with the generous, child-friendly layout.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Expected pattern: stacked vertical forms with `<label>` elements above `<input>`/`<textarea>` controls (`<div class="form-group">` wrappers). Sections of longer forms might use `<fieldset>` and `<legend>` for semantic grouping. Call-to-action buttons would appear below fields, full-width or left-aligned within the form container.

ui_patterns.visual_description: The main site navigation is not fully visible in the provided frame, but given the municipal/childcare context and the visible hero, it is likely a horizontal top nav above or integrated with the hero. Styling would probably use simple text links in dark brown on the light cream background, with sufficient horizontal spacing and possibly a subtle hover underline or color change to the brand yellow or orange. Active states might be indicated by bold weight and/or a bottom border bar.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern: Expected structure: a semantic `<nav>` element containing an unordered list `<ul>` of list items `<li>`, each wrapping an `<a>` link. The nav may be inside a header `<header>` along with logo and possibly language switcher. On mobile, this could collapse into a hamburger menu with a toggle button controlling a vertical menu list.

ui_patterns.visual_description: The welcome section uses a vertical numbered list with custom styling. Each list item consists of a large yellow circle (~40–48px diameter) with a centered white number in bold text. Circles likely have soft drop shadows or subtle darker edges for depth. To the right of each circle, the corresponding paragraph is left-aligned in a standard text column. Vertical spacing between items is generous (~16–24px), and there are no visible horizontal separators; separation relies on spacing and the numbered badges. This yields a clean, step-by-step narrative feel.
ui_patterns.structural_pattern:
Structurally, it could be an ordered list `
    ` with list items styled using pseudo-elements (e.g., `::before` for the circle), or a series of flexbox rows: `
    1
    …
    `. The visual numbering suggests semantic `
      ` would be appropriate, but a custom flex layout is also common for this design.

heading.visual_style: Playful, heavy, rounded display sans-serif with thick strokes and fairly large counters. It has a childlike, friendly character reminiscent of cartoon or children’s book titles, with low contrast and strong presence.

body.visual_style: Clean, humanist sans-serif with good legibility and moderate stroke contrast. Appears more neutral than headings but still warm; likely slightly rounded terminals and generous x-height suitable for paragraphs.

font_families.code: Source Code Pro, Menlo, Monaco, Consolas, "Courier New", monospace

font_sizes.h1: 40px–48px (hero headline even larger, ~56px–64px in the slider)

spacing_system.component_padding: Buttons and badges: 8–12px internal padding around content. Cards/hero sections: 24–32px padding on all sides. Text blocks inside sections: top/bottom padding of ~16–24px from headings and list items.
spacing_system.section_margin: Vertical spacing between major sections of approximately 48–64px, with inner content often separated by 24–32px margins.

interaction_hints: Slider arrows likely use hover states with increased opacity, slight background color shift (darker beige or more saturated yellow), and perhaps a subtle scale-up animation (e.g., transform: scale(1.05)).
Scroll-to-top button likely changes background brightness or arrow color on hover/focus and may have a smooth scrolling behavior when clicked (CSS scroll-behavior or JS animation).
Links and interactive text (in nav or body) are expected to change color to the brand yellow/orange and/or gain an underline on hover/focus, improving affordance.
Transitions between hover states are likely subtle and eased (150–250ms) to maintain a gentle, child-friendly feeling rather than snappy, abrupt changes.
Micro-interactions for list items or cards could include slightly lifting shadows or translating upward by 1–2px on hover to give a tactile, toy-like feedback.
